title: Vendor access telemetry — principals active outside contracted hours/geos
exclusions: Does NOT measure vendor-supplied service outages; focuses on vendor principals acting against the estate.
evidence: Saved search 'vendor_access_off_contract' (hourly) emits vendor principals acting outside contracted windows or permitted geographies.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.28 ICT third-party oversight explicitly requires telemetry of third-party access.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.28 (ICT third-party risk) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.44.4: Vendor access telemetry — principals active outside contracted hours/geos.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.44.4' running on IdP authentication events for named vendor principals, vendor contracts lookup with contracted hours/geos., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) (Supply-chain security) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.44.4: Vendor access telemetry — principals active outside contracted hours/geos.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.44.4' running on IdP authentication events for named vendor principals, vendor contracts lookup with contracted hours/geos., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: SR-6 supplier assessments and reviews include telemetry.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST-800-53 SR-6 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.44.4: Vendor access telemetry — principals active outside contracted hours/geos.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.44.4' running on IdP authentication events for named vendor principals, vendor contracts lookup with contracted hours/geos., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Vendor principal vendor_abc@x logs in at 03:00 UTC on Sunday from a geography not on the contract's permitted list; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Same principal during contracted weekday hours from the expected geography; no alert.

dataSources: IdP authentication events for named vendor principals, vendor contracts lookup with contracted hours/geos.
spl:
index=idp earliest=-1h (sourcetype=okta:audit OR sourcetype=azuread:audit) result=success
| search user IN (`vendor_principals`)
| `get_geolocation(src_ip)`
| lookup vendor_contract.csv user OUTPUT allowed_hours allowed_countries
| eval hour=strftime(_time,"%H"), weekday=strftime(_time,"%A"), window_ok=if(match(allowed_hours,weekday.":".hour),1,0), geo_ok=if(match(allowed_countries,country),1,0)
| where window_ok=0 OR geo_ok=0
| table _time user country hour weekday allowed_countries allowed_hours
description: Intersects vendor principal authentication events with contracted access windows (hours/days) and permitted geographies; divergence is a DORA Art.28 finding.
value: Materialises contractual access controls into operational telemetry.
implementation: (1) Maintain vendor_principals macro; (2) extract contracted windows/geos from contracts and publish to vendor_contract.csv; (3) run hourly. Indexes required: `index=idp`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=azuread:audit`, `sourcetype=okta:audit`.
visualization: World map of vendor logins with off-geo markers, bar chart of vendors over time, single-value 'vendor logins within contract (24h)'. Key fields: `allowed_countries`, `allowed_hours`, `country`, `hour`, `user`, `weekday`.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1078.004 Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts

knownFalsePositives: Planned after-hours maintenance windows need a pre-declared exception with vendor_contract.exception_id.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for intersects vendor principal authentication events with contracted access windows (hours/days) and permitted geographies; divergence is a dora finding. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: IdP authentication events for named vendor principals, vendor contracts lookup with contracted hours/geos..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain vendor_principals macro; (2) extract contracted windows/geos from contracts and publish to vendor_contract.csv; (3) run hourly.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=idp earliest=-1h (sourcetype=okta:audit OR sourcetype=azuread:audit) result=success
| search user IN (`vendor_principals`)
| `get_geolocation(src_ip)`
| lookup vendor_contract.csv user OUTPUT allowed_hours allowed_countries
| eval hour=strftime(_time,"%H"), weekday=strftime(_time,"%A"), window_ok=if(match(allowed_hours,weekday.":".hour),1,0), geo_ok=if(match(allowed_countries,country),1,0)
| where window_ok=0 OR geo_ok=0
| table _time user country hour weekday allowed_countries allowed_hours
```

Understanding this SPL

**Vendor access telemetry — principals active outside contracted hours/geos** — Materialises contractual access controls into operational telemetry.

Documented **Data sources**: IdP authentication events for named vendor principals, vendor contracts lookup with contracted hours/geos. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: idp; **sourcetype**: okta:audit, azuread:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=idp, sourcetype=okta:audit, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Invokes macro `get_geolocation(src_ip)` — in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hour** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where window_ok=0 OR geo_ok=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, allowed_countries, allowed_hours, country, hour, user, weekday.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: World map of vendor logins with off-geo markers, bar chart of vendors over time, single-value 'vendor logins within contract (24h)'.
